junction_vs_copy_decision_hint.my_recommendation_as_test_pc_claude: Junction (mklink /J) is the right primitive here. No admin, no Developer Mode, works on NTFS directories, and each ~/.claude/skills/<skill>/ is always a directory so per-file symlink granularity is not needed. One-time setup, then `git pull` alone keeps skills live — no extra step, no drift, no sync script to maintain or forget.

desktop_commander_status.evidence: No mcp__desktop-commander__* tools registered in this session's tool surface. Tools available for system actions: Bash (git-bash), PowerShell (dedicated tool), windows-mcp (Click/Type/Screenshot/PowerShell/FileSystem — available via ToolSearch).
desktop_commander_status.impact_on_worker: The test-pc-worker SKILL.md references mcp__desktop-commander__* as its allowed-tools. On THIS session those specific tools are absent — test-pc-worker would fall back to PowerShell/Bash if invoked. If dev's sync strategy depends on desktop-commander, please confirm whether to install it or migrate test-pc-worker's tool list to use windows-mcp / built-in Bash+PowerShell.

options_evaluated.name: Developer Mode ON + symlink
options_evaluated.pros: Works for directories AND files; standard POSIX-like behavior.
options_evaluated.cons: Requires Developer Mode toggle (user-visible system setting, one-time UI click); Developer Mode also enables other dev features user may not want; symlinks are sometimes treated differently by AV/backup software.

options_evaluated.name: Junction (mklink /J)
options_evaluated.pros: No admin, no Developer Mode, no extra software. `git pull` instantly visible to Claude. Simplest possible live-sync. NTFS-native, rock-solid.
options_evaluated.cons: Directories only (fine here — skills are always directories). `rm -rf` inside the junction deletes the target too, so any cleanup script must unlink first (cmd: `rmdir` on the junction itself, not `rm -rf`).

options_evaluated.name: Manual copy script after pull
options_evaluated.cons: Easy to forget; drift window between pull and copy; requires running on every pull.

options_evaluated.name: Git post-merge hook + auto-copy
options_evaluated.pros: Automatic on every pull; explicit copy avoids junction edge cases.
options_evaluated.cons: Hook lives in .git/hooks (not versioned — each clone must install separately); silent failure modes; copy is O(skills) instead of O(1) like junction.

preferred_sync_mechanism.recommendation: Option B (Junction) — best fit for Windows + NTFS + DevMode OFF, 1-time setup, zero ongoing maintenance. Option D (post-merge hook auto-copy) is the clean fallback if junction causes issues with any tool that dislikes reparse points.
preferred_sync_mechanism.proposed_setup_command_sketch: for d in $(ls /c/Users/최장희/Documents/dev_test_sync/shared-skills/); do if [ -d "/c/Users/최장희/Documents/dev_test_sync/shared-skills/$d" ]; then src="C:\Users\최장희\Documents\dev_test_sync\shared-skills\$d"; dst="C:\Users\최장희\.claude\skills\$d"; rmdir "$dst" 2>/dev/null; cmd.exe //c "mklink /J \"$dst\" \"$src\""; fi; done
preferred_sync_mechanism.caveat_for_cleanup: If later removing a junction, use `cmd /c rmdir <junction>` — NOT `rm -rf`, which would recurse into and delete the source.

result.outcome: Phase A env profile complete. Key findings: (1) Windows 11 24H2 build 26200, NTFS, DevMode OFF. (2) All 11 installed skills are frozen plain-directory copies from 2026-04-14, missing today's type declarations — confirms prior stale-install diagnosis. (3) Junction is the strongest sync mechanism available without admin/DevMode toggle. (4) desktop-commander MCP is NOT present in this session — test-pc-worker's allowed-tools list may need revision, or MCP needs to be installed.
